The organization(s) to which the person belongs in the context of this dataset. May vary across datasets; multivalued to support multiple affiliations.
The email address of the person. Represents current/preferred contact information in the context of this dataset.
ORCID (Open Researcher and Contributor ID) - a persistent digital identifier for researchers. Format: 0000-0000-0000-0000 (16 digits in groups of 4). Use this for stable cross-dataset identification.
Whether quotes within quoted fields are escaped by doubling them.
Whether the first row contains column headers (true/false).

References to one or more SamplingStrategy objects.

Indicates whether it is a sample of a larger set.
Indicates whether the sample is random.
Description of the larger set from which the sample was drawn, if any.
Indicates whether the sample is representative of the larger set.
Explanation of how representativeness was validated or verified.
Explanation of why the sample is not representative, if applicable.
Description of the sampling strategy (deterministic, probabilistic, etc.).
Description of the missing data fields or elements.
Explanation of why each piece of data is missing.
Details on recommended data splits and their rationale.
Details on errors, noise sources, or redundancies in the dataset.
The type of bias identified, using standardized categories from the Artificial Intelligence Ontology (AIO).
Detailed description of how this bias manifests in the dataset, including affected populations, features, or outcomes.
Steps taken or recommended to mitigate this bias.
Specific subsets or features of the dataset affected by this bias.
Detailed description of the limitation and its implications.
How this limitation affects the scope or applicability of the dataset.
Recommended approaches for users to address this limitation.
Explanation of any commitments that external resources will remain available and stable over time.
Indication whether official archival versions of external resources are included.
Description of any restrictions or fees associated with external resources.
Indicates whether any confidential data elements are present.
Details on confidential data elements and handling procedures.
Indicates whether any content warnings are needed.
Indicates whether any subpopulations are explicitly identified.
The distribution of instances across identified subpopulations, including counts, percentages, or proportions for each subgroup.
Indicates whether data subjects can be identified.
List of identifier types removed during de-identification.
Details on de-identification procedures and residual risks.
Indicates whether sensitive data elements are present.
Details on sensitive data elements present and handling procedures.
The dataset that this relationship points to. Can be specified by identifier, URL, or Dataset object.
Free-text description providing additional context about the relationship.
Description of the dataset's license and terms of use (including links, costs, or usage constraints).

Structured data use permissions using the Data Use Ontology (DUO). Specifies permitted uses (e.g., general research, health/medical research, disease-specific research) and restrictions (e.g., non-commercial use, ethics approval required, collaboration required). See https://github.com/EBISPOT/DUO

Contact person for licensing questions. Provides structured contact information including name, email, affiliation, and optional ORCID. This person can answer questions about licensing terms, usage restrictions, fees, and permissions.
Explanation of third-party IP restrictions.
Export or regulatory restrictions on the dataset.
Indicates compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). HIPAA applies to protected health information in the United States.
Confidentiality classification of the dataset indicating level of access restrictions and sensitivity.
Contact person for data governance committee. This person can answer questions about data governance policies, access procedures, and oversight mechanisms.
Boolean indicating whether the dataset is distributed to parties external to the dataset-creating entity.
Details of the distribution channel(s) or format(s).
Dates or timeframe for dataset release. Could be a one-time release date or multiple scheduled releases.
Contact person for questions about ethical review. Provides structured contact information including name, email, affiliation, and optional ORCID.
Details on ethical review processes, outcomes, and supporting documentation.
Details on data protection impact analysis, outcomes, and documentation.
Details on how individuals were notified about data collection.
Details on how consent was requested, provided, and documented.
Details on consent revocation mechanisms and procedures.

Representative score typically achieved in areas demonstrating this strength.

Comprehensive explanation of the insight and its implications.
Quantitative or qualitative evidence supporting this analytical insight.

Supplementary materials
Does this dataset involve human subjects research?
Does this dataset involve human subjects research?
Was Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval obtained? Include approval number and institution if applicable.
What ethics review board(s) reviewed this research? Include institution names and approval details.
Does the research involve any special populations that require additional protections (e.g., minors, pregnant women, prisoners)?
What regulatory frameworks govern this human subjects research (e.g., 45 CFR 46, HIPAA)?
Was informed consent obtained from all participants?
Was informed consent obtained from all participants?
What type of consent was obtained (e.g., written, verbal, electronic, implied through participation)?
How is consent documented? Include references to consent forms or procedures used.
How can participants withdraw their consent? What procedures are in place for data deletion upon withdrawal?
How can participants withdraw their consent? What procedures are in place for data deletion upon withdrawal?
What specific uses did participants consent to? Are there limitations on data use based on consent?
What specific uses did participants consent to? Are there limitations on data use based on consent?
What methods were used to anonymize or de-identify participant data? Include technical details of privacy-preserving techniques.
What is the assessed risk of re-identification? What measures were taken to minimize this risk?
What is the assessed risk of re-identification? What measures were taken to minimize this risk?
What privacy-preserving techniques were applied (e.g., differential privacy, k-anonymity, data masking)?
Can this dataset be linked to other datasets in ways that might compromise participant privacy?
Can this dataset be linked to other datasets in ways that might compromise participant privacy?
Were participants compensated for their participation?
Were participants compensated for their participation?
What type of compensation was provided (e.g., monetary payment, gift cards, course credit, other incentives)?
What was the amount or value of compensation provided? Include currency or equivalent value.
What was the rationale for the compensation structure? How was the amount determined to be appropriate?
What was the rationale for the compensation structure? How was the amount determined to be appropriate?
Are any at-risk populations included (e.g., children, pregnant women, prisoners, cognitively impaired individuals)?
What additional protections were implemented for at-risk populations? Include safeguards, modified procedures, or additional oversight.
For research involving minors, what assent procedures were used? How was developmentally appropriate assent obtained?
For research involving minors, what assent procedures were used? How was developmentally appropriate assent obtained?
For participants unable to provide their own consent, how was guardian or surrogate consent obtained?
For participants unable to provide their own consent, how was guardian or surrogate consent obtained?
Details on who will support, host, or maintain the dataset.
URL or access point for the erratum.
Details on any errata or corrections to the dataset.
Details on update plans, responsible parties, and communication methods.
Time period for data retention.
Details on data retention limits and enforcement procedures.
DOI or URL of the latest dataset version.
List of available versions with metadata.
Details on version support policies and obsolescence communication.
URL for contribution guidelines or process.
Details on extension mechanisms, contribution validation, and communication.
The datasets in this collection.
Short explanation describing the primary purpose of creating the dataset.
Short explanation describing the specific task or tasks for which this dataset was created.
Short explanation of the knowledge or resource gap that this dataset was intended to address.
A key individual (Principal Investigator) responsible for or overseeing dataset creation.
Organizations with which the creator or team is affiliated.
Name/identifier of the organization providing monetary or resource support.
Grant mechanisms supporting dataset creation. Multiple grants may fund a single dataset.
The alphanumeric identifier for the grant.
Details on preprocessing steps applied to the data.
Details on data cleaning procedures applied.
Annotation methodology, tasks, and protocols followed during labeling. Includes annotation guidelines, quality control procedures, and task definitions.
Number of annotations collected per data item. Multiple annotations per item enable calculation of inter-annotator agreement.
Details on labeling/annotation procedures and quality metrics.
URL or access point for the raw data.
Details on raw data availability and access procedures.
Specific imputation technique used (mean, median, mode, forward fill, backward fill, interpolation, model-based imputation, etc.).
Fields or columns where imputation was applied.
Justification for the imputation approach chosen, including assumptions made about missing data mechanisms.
Methods used to validate imputation quality (if any).
Type of agreement metric used (Cohen's kappa, Fleiss' kappa, Krippendorff's alpha, percentage agreement, etc.).
Methodology used to assess annotation quality and resolve disagreements.
Additional details on annotation quality assessment and findings.
Descriptions of what each tool does in the annotation process and what types of annotations it produces. Should correspond to the tools list.
List of examples of known/previous uses of the dataset.
URL to a repository of known dataset uses.
Details on the repository of known dataset uses.
Details on other potential tasks the dataset could be used for.
Details on potential impacts, risks, and mitigation strategies.
Details on tasks for which the dataset should not be used.
List of example intended uses for this dataset.
Notes or caveats about using the dataset for intended purposes.
The name or identifier of the variable as it appears in the data files.
The unit of measurement for the variable, preferably using QUDT units (http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/). Examples: qudt:Kilogram, qudt:Meter, qudt:DegreeCelsius.
Code(s) used to represent missing values for this variable. Examples: "NA", "-999", "null", "". Multiple codes may be specified.
The minimum value that the variable can take. Applicable to numeric variables.
The maximum value that the variable can take. Applicable to numeric variables.
The permitted categories or values for a categorical variable. Each entry should describe a possible value and its meaning.
Example values for this variable to illustrate typical data.
Indicates whether this variable serves as a unique identifier or key for records in the dataset.
The precision or number of decimal places for numeric variables.
The technique or method used to measure this variable. Examples: "mass spectrometry", "self-report survey", "GPS coordinates".
Description of how this variable was derived or calculated from other variables, if applicable.
Notes about data quality, reliability, or known issues specific to this variable.
Whole numbers.
Text strings.
True/false values.
